name: station_physics_validation
discovered: 2026-07-02, AlohaMini Pro nav pick-and-place after re-enabling base<->table collision

symptoms: the robot base visually overlaps/penetrates furniture at its manipulation station
base cannot hold the commanded station pose: it drifts or gets shoved during manipulation
manipulation succeeded only because obstacle<->base collision had been hacked off
trace_evidence: candidate station lies inside an obstacle footprint (table slab x[-0.43,0.17] y[-0.69,-0.21] in the AlohaMini scene)
after teleporting to the station and stepping physics, |base_actual - base_target| grows above ~2 cm
contact pairs base_link/vertical_link <-> table appear with large impulse

when_to_apply: Whenever selecting a mobile-base station near obstacles — a kinematically-perfect station is worthless if physics rejects it.
repair_strategy: Gate every candidate station with a physics settle-check: teleport the base joints there, hold the pose for a few control steps, and reject the candidate if the base got pushed off target (obstacle contact) before even scoring its IK. Never disable obstacle collision to force a station; move the TARGET (or the object) into range of a legal station instead.
code_sketch: for _ in range(6): env.step(hold_action)      # physics votes on the station
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro nav pick-and-place with real base<->table collision (bit-24 hack removed)
